raw_brief:
PRODUCT: Pixiedining — a restaurant availability notifier for Walt Disney
World and Disneyland. Disney's official dining reservation system opens 60 days
ahead and the most desirable tables (Be Our Guest, Cinderella's Royal Table,
Space 220, Storybook Dining, Topolino's Terrace breakfast, etc.) book out in
seconds. Cancellations trickle back into the pool unpredictably for weeks.
Pixiedining monitors the official availability feed and sends real-time alerts
the moment a slot matching the user's saved criteria opens up, so users can
pivot back to the official Disney site and grab it before someone else does.

PURPOSE: Convert visitors into paying users for two concrete outcomes —
(a) free signups for a single-restaurant alert during one upcoming trip, and
(b) paid Pro subscriptions ($14.99/mo or $99/yr) for unlimited restaurants,
multi-park bundles, push + SMS notifications, faster polling cadence, and
auto-resume after the alert fires. The site must establish credibility quickly
because users are skeptical of any third-party that touches Disney's reservation
system, and emotionally invested because the trip itself is often a once-a-year
or once-in-a-lifetime expense.

AUDIENCE: Three segments that should self-select.

  Segment A — One-trip family planner:
    Who: A parent or trip-planner organizing a 5-10 day Disney vacation, often
      6-12 months out. Already booked the resort and park tickets; dining is
      the last anxiety.
    Knowledge: Knows Disney's 60-day dining window exists. Has tried refreshing
      the Disney app. Doesn't know if third-party alert services are safe or
      legitimate.
    Decision behavior: Decides in under two minutes whether the site looks like
      a legitimate, U.S.-based service. Trust signals (volume of alerts sent,
      example success stories, "we never log into your Disney account",
      payment processor logos, refund guarantee) close the gap. Will sign up
      free first, upgrade only if the free alert proves the service is real.

  Segment B — Disney superfan / Annual Passholder:
    Who: Visits multiple times per year, plans intricate dining itineraries,
      participates in Disney Reddit and Facebook groups.
    Knowledge: Expert. Already knows about competitor tools (MouseDining,
      DVC Reservation Finder, Touring Plans). Cares about polling cadence,
      restaurant coverage breadth, alert latency, and notification channels.
    Decision behavior: Methodical. Compares against alternatives. Will pay
      annual upfront if convinced the service has lower latency, broader
      coverage, and SMS support that competitors lack.

  Segment C — Travel agent / planner:
    Who: A Disney-specialized travel agent (Magical Vacations, Mickey Travels,
      Earmarked Vacations) booking dining for many client families per month.
    Knowledge: Expert. Needs reliability and ability to monitor multiple
      simultaneous client trips at once.
    Decision behavior: Wants a clear team/agency tier with multi-account or
      multi-trip management; will inquire via a contact path before paying.

CONTEXT: Standalone marketing landing page (single page, root of pixiedining.com).
  Entry: paid search ("disney dining reservation finder", "be our guest
    cancellation alert"), Reddit r/WaltDisneyWorld referral threads, TikTok
    discovery videos.
  Exit: signup flow for free single-restaurant alert (primary), Stripe checkout
    for Pro subscription (secondary), or contact form for travel agent tier
    (tertiary).
  User journey stage: SELLING. Pre-purchase for both free and paid tiers.
    Trust establishment + emotional reassurance ("we'll watch for that table
    so you don't have to refresh at midnight") are the primary blockers.

KEY ACTIONS:
  1. PRIMARY — Start a free single-restaurant alert
     (enter trip dates + party size + restaurant → email/push).
  2. SECONDARY — Upgrade to Pro for unlimited restaurants, SMS, faster polling.
  3. TERTIARY — See a sample alert / how it works / coverage list — also a
     Travel Agent Plan inquiry path for Segment C.
optional_business_context: Subscription SaaS with a free alert as a hook. Pro at $14.99/mo or $99/yr. Travel agent tier inquiry-based. Stripe checkout. No login to Disney accounts — only public availability feed monitored.
optional_known_constraints: Must establish trust quickly (third-party service touching Disney reservations). No claim of guaranteed booking — only earlier notification. Mobile-first because users plan on phones during/after vacation.
optional_user_preferences: Warm, slightly magical visual register without becoming a Disney clone. Functional clarity beats lifestyle storytelling — these users want proof the alerts work, not whimsy.
optional_existing_site_or_brand_notes: No existing brand system. Domain pixiedining.com. Compete with MouseDining, Touring Plans dining widget, and DVC Reservation Finder.
